description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a large, sloped red roof made of corrugated metal or similar material. The structure is primarily constructed from light-colored wooden planks, giving it a rustic and functional appearance. One side of the building is open, supported by wooden posts, likely serving as an entry or sheltered area for livestock. The other sides are fully enclosed, with no visible windows, and the roof extends over the entire structure, providing ample protection from the elements. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative features, focusing on practicality and durability. The building is substantial in size, suitable for housing multiple cows or other livestock, and is typical of rural or peri-urban agricultural settings.
description: A large, rectangular wooden cowshed with a sloped red roof and one open side supported by posts. The structure is utilitarian and designed for livestock shelter.
description_view_0: The open side of the cowshed is visible, supported by wooden posts, with the red sloped roof extending overhead.
description_view_1: The gable end of the cowshed is shown, fully enclosed with wooden planks and the red roof forming a triangular peak.
description_view_2: A long, fully enclosed wooden side of the cowshed is visible beneath the expansive red roof.
description_view_3: The opposite gable end is shown, also fully enclosed with wooden planks and the red roof sloping down to the sides.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door, sliding or hinged
